sources.name: rocprofiler-sdk Examples
sources.license: MIT-style; verify current license at pinned commit

sources.name: FlashInfer Sampling Documentation
sources.license: FlashInfer documentation and repository terms; cite and summarize unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for Top-K, Top-P, min-p, logits/probability sampling API contracts, deterministic mode, seed handling, and HIP Graph compatibility notes.
sources.notes: Primary public docs for FlashInfer sampling APIs referenced by the Top-K roadmap.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.topk Documentation
sources.license: PyTorch documentation terms; cite and summarize unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for framework Top-K baseline contract, sorted flag behavior, output shape, and caveats.
sources.notes: Practical oracle and baseline entry point for Top-K comparisons.

sources.name: PyTorch CUDA Top-K Dispatch Source
sources.license: PyTorch BSD-style license; verify upstream license before copying code
sources.use_policy: Use for ATen CUDA Top-K dispatch, selected-result sorting behavior, full-sort fallback checks, and framework baseline implementation notes. Treat this as CUDA-origin/PyTorch GPU-source context only; do not cite it as ROCm measurement evidence.
sources.notes: Shows dispatch to gather Top-K and selected-result sort when sorted output is requested.

sources.name: PyTorch CUDA Top-K Kernel Source
sources.license: PyTorch BSD-style license; verify upstream license before copying code
sources.use_policy: Use for radix kth-value selection, single-block and multi-block gather paths, and PyTorch Top-K kernel implementation notes. Treat this as CUDA-origin/PyTorch GPU-source context only; do not cite it as ROCm measurement evidence.
sources.notes: Primary GPU source for PyTorch Top-K selection and gather implementation.

sources.name: FlashInfer Top-K Documentation
sources.license: FlashInfer documentation and repository terms; cite and summarize unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for FlashInfer Top-K API contract, sorted/deterministic/tie-break flags, small-vocab caveats, and radix selection notes.
sources.notes: Top-K-specific documentation complementing the FlashInfer sampling API docs.

sources.name: vLLM Top-K Top-P Triton Source
sources.license: Apache-2.0
sources.use_policy: Use for vLLM Qrita-inspired pivot masking, duplicate-boundary handling, and Top-K/Top-P dispatch implementation notes.
sources.notes: Important because vLLM may use masking/pivot selection rather than materialized sorted Top-K.

sources.name: OneFlow CUDA Top-K Source
sources.license: Apache-2.0
sources.use_policy: Use for heap-based small-k Top-K, full-sort fallback, and framework Top-K implementation comparison.
sources.notes: Compact reference for heap versus hipCUB sort crossover.

sources.name: Transformer Engine Fused Router Top-K Source
sources.license: BSD-3-Clause
sources.use_policy: Use for MoE expert-routing Top-K, grouped Top-K, score-function fusion, and forward/backward routing behavior.
sources.notes: MoE routing reference, not a generic row-major vocabulary Top-K baseline.

sources.name: Qrita: High-performance Top-k and Top-p Algorithm for GPUs using Pivot-based Truncation and Selection
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use as a research target for deterministic pivot/truncation Top-K and Top-P algorithms and reproduction planning.
sources.notes: 2026 paper reporting deterministic Top-K/Top-P speedups versus serving-stack baselines.

sources.name: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for exact sampling and fused matmul-sample design ideas when the Top-K project can move the boundary before materialized logits.
sources.notes: Relevant to decode-regime sampling and logits materialization boundaries.

sources.name: RTop-K: Ultra-Fast Row-Wise Top-K Algorithm and GPU Implementation for Neural Networks
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for row-wise Top-K algorithm ideas and pivot/search alternatives to sort-based baselines.
sources.notes: Useful background for row-wise Top-K regimes beyond tiny fixed-k insertion lists.

sources.name: RadiK: Scalable and Optimized GPU-Parallel Radix Top-K Selection
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for medium and large-k radix-selection design and scalability comparisons.
sources.notes: Research reference for larger k, input length, and batch-size scalability.

sources.name: Billion-scale Similarity Search with GPUs
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use as classic background for register-heavy GPU k-selection; do not treat as an LLM sampling baseline without matching the contract.
sources.notes: Classic FAISS GPU k-selection reference for small-k thinking.

sources.name: Split-bucket partition: a novel execution model for top-K and selection algorithms on GPUs
sources.license: Open-access article; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for bucket-partition Top-K execution models, bucket/select/merge decomposition, and fused bucket-selection experiment design.
sources.notes: Useful grounding for split-bucket and score-bin wild ideas.

sources.name: Foundations of Top-k Decoding For Language Models
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for Top-K decoding policy semantics and policy-level comparisons separate from exact CUDA Top-K selection.
sources.notes: Policy reference; not a HIP kernel baseline.

sources.name: Decoding as Optimisation on the Probability Simplex: From Top-K to Top-P (Nucleus) to Best-of-K Samplers
sources.license: Paper metadata page; cite and summarize unless full license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for contract-changing decoding-policy experiments such as simplex/projection threshold kernels, not as exact Top-K evidence.
sources.notes: Supports the sparsemax/simplex decoder hypothesis as a policy experiment.

sources.name: Kascade: A Practical Sparse Attention Method for Long-Context LLM Inference
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for sparse-attention Top-K index reuse ideas, with explicit quality and exactness labels.
sources.notes: Relevant to cross-layer Top-K index reuse for sparse attention.

sources.name: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse
sources.license: arXiv paper; metadata and citations only unless license is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for cross-layer sparse-attention Top-K index reuse experiments and trace-based validation plans.
sources.notes: Supports reuse-layer and repair-rate experiments in the Top-K wild ideas lab.

sources.name: PyTorch torch Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for public torch API categories, function names, and reference links for PyTorch-baseline challenge tasks.
sources.notes: Stable PyTorch docs redirected to 2.12 on 2026-05-21 in this environment.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.nn.functional Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for public neural-network functional APIs such as convolution, pooling, attention, activation, normalization, and losses.
sources.notes: Primary public contract source for functional neural-network ops in PyTorch challenge references.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.nn Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for module-level public contracts such as Conv, Linear, MultiheadAttention, Transformer, and normalization modules.
sources.notes: Module API reference for constructing PyTorch baseline models.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.linalg Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for public dense linear algebra semantics and solver/decomposition baseline tasks.
sources.notes: Linear algebra public API reference for solver and decomposition challenge ideas.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.fft Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for public FFT and spectral operation contracts, especially audio and signal challenge tasks.
sources.notes: FFT public API reference for fixed-size signal and audio kernels.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.sparse Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for sparse tensor layout invariants, sparse operation contracts, and semi-structured sparse challenge tasks.
sources.notes: Sparse public API reference, including CUDA-origin semi-structured sparse constraints.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.special Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for special-function public API semantics and special-math HIP/ROCm challenge categories.
sources.notes: Special mathematical functions are mostly pointwise but can dominate probability/statistics workloads.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.masked Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for masked operation contracts, masked reductions, and mask semantics in challenge tasks.
sources.notes: Masked APIs are useful public contracts for fused mask and reduction kernels.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.nested Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for nested/ragged tensor public semantics and ragged batch challenge design.
sources.notes: Nested tensor APIs provide public contracts for ragged sequence and variable-length workloads.

sources.name: PyTorch torch.distributions Reference API
sources.license: PyTorch documentation license; cite and summarize unless copying is reviewed
sources.use_policy: Use for distribution sampling/log-prob contracts and correctness policies for sampling challenges.
sources.notes: Distribution APIs matter for sampling tasks where exact replay and distributional correctness differ.
